column18: Best for: Professionals wanting easy scheduling. Market leader
column20: Limited group events on free tier, SMS reminders cost extra, can't remove Calendly branding on free tier, can get expensive for teams, limited customization on free.
column21: TESTED: Used for client scheduling. Setup was easy (connected to Google Calendar instantly). Clients loved it (could book without back-and-forth). However, hit free tier limitations fast (1 event type only). Group events required upgrade to Professional ($16/month). SMS reminders were extra cost. Perfect for solopreneurs, professional teams should consider Cal.com for better pricing.

column19: Best for: Service businesses needing appointment scheduling. Robust but can be expensive.
column20: More expensive than competitors, learning curve for advanced features, can feel overwhelming with options, limited customization on lower tiers, UI feels dated.
column21: TESTED: Set up scheduling for coaching business. Intake forms and appointment types were robust (could collect custom info per appointment type). Reminder system was excellent (email + SMS). However, at $15/month for Starter, more expensive than Calendly. UI felt dated compared to Calendly/Cal.com. Perfect for service businesses needing robust scheduling, Calendly is simpler/cheaper.

column14: Best for: Open-source advocates wanting white-label scheduling. Best value and customizability.
column15: Newer platform (fewer integrations), some features still in development, smaller template library, community support (no phone support), requires more setup for advanced features.
column16: TESTED: Used for podcast booking. Open-source approach meant full customization (white-label, no branding). Free tier was incredible (unlimited bookings!). However, integrations were fewer than Calendly (couldn't connect to CRM). Some features (SMS reminders, more payment gateways) were still in development. Perfect if you want white-label and customizability, Calendly is better if you need mature features.

column17: Best for: Budget-conscious individuals. Cheapest option with solid features.
column18: Limited customization (UI feels dated), no SMS reminders, fewer integrations, can feel basic compared to competitors, group events basic.
column19: TESTED: Used for consulting calls. Setup was straightforward. Pricing was excellent ($10/month for Plus). Email reminders worked well. However, customization was limited (couldn't match brand perfectly). No SMS reminders (email only). UI felt dated compared to modern alternatives. Perfect for budget-conscious solopreneurs, Calendly is better if you want polish.

column18: Best for: Service businesses with complex scheduling needs. Most features but can be complex.
column19: UI can be confusing (too many options), steep learning curve, can feel overwhelming, setup takes longer, pricing tiers restrictive (feature limits).
column20: TESTED: Set up appointment scheduling for spa business. Features were incredible (could accept deposits, sell packages, manage staff). Payment processing was flexible (multiple gateways). However, setup took 4+ hours (so many options to configure). UI was confusing (too many settings, poor organization). Perfect for businesses needing complex scheduling, Calendly is better for simplicity.
